phases.title: Enneahcube local alphabet
phases.objective: Define local admissible state alphabet; do not mistake it for the whole cosmology.

phases.title: Branch ontology
phases.objective: Define branch histories as first-class cosmological objects.
proof_targets: admissible child states or explicit defects
branch count law when finite branching factor exists
existence of countable or measurable tree
committed history is a path in 𝒯
failure_conditions: branch layer treated as optional decoration

phases.title: Branch measure or amplitude law
proof_targets: Born rule or explicit replacement if quantum-like

phases.title: Observer commitment and memory
phases.objective: Define observation as branch-relative memory, not necessarily collapse.
proof_targets: observer record lies on an admissible branch
other branches persist under many-worlds interpretation
failure_conditions: observer record not connected to branch tree

phases.title: Decoherence and branch separation
phases.objective: Make leaf branches world-like through separation/orthogonality/decoherence.
proof_targets: branch memory divergence causes effective non-interference
failure_conditions: branches remain mutually interfering without a rule
no distinction between computational candidates and worlds

phases.title: Branch-to-spacetime bridge
phases.objective: Map branch ensembles, not only finite states, to effective physical quantities.
proof_targets: branch ensemble produces stable coarse variables
observables are computable from ASH definitions
failure_conditions: bridge depends only on X_t and ignores branches

phases.title: Emergent geometry and effective physics
phases.objective: Derive metric, fields, and dynamical equations or a coherent alternative.
proof_targets: bounded Lorentz violations or emergent symmetry

phases.title: Cosmological observables
phases.objective: Produce measurable predictions from the fixed branch-centered ASH theory.
failure_conditions: only fits one data sector by breaking another
